name: Sierra de Ricote y La Navela Loop
name_original: Cieza, Ermita Virgen del Buen Suceso, El Menjú, Las Norias – Ruta Circular – Sierra de Ricote y La Navela
description: This difficult 34.3-mile mountain biking route offers panoramic views and challenging terrain in Spain's Sierra de Ricote y La Navela.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cieza offers diverse natural landscapes, from rugged mountains to lush river valleys and extensive orchards. The region is characterized by the Segura River carving through canyons like Almadenes, and the challenging terrain of the Sierra de Ricote. Ancient irrigation canals, known as acequias, also provide scenic paths through traditional agricultural areas. This varied topography ensures a wide range of mountain bike trails with significant elevation changes and mixed surfaces. Best mountain bike trails around Cieza The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Ricote y La Navela Loop, a 34.3 miles (55.3 km) trail that takes 6 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers challenging terrain and panoramic views across the Sierra de Ricote. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Puente de Alambre – Ricote Valley loop from Cieza, a moderate 32.1 miles (51.6 km) path. This route features moderate elevation changes as it winds through the scenic Ricote Valley. Local mountain bikers also love the Acequia de la Andelma – Puente de Alambre loop from Cieza, a 23.6 miles (37.9 km) trail leading through ancient irrigation canals and orchards, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Mountain biking around Cieza is defined by rugged mountains, river valleys, and ancient irrigation canals. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to very challenging. The routes in Cieza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cieza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Cieza?
A: Mountain biking around Cieza offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find rugged mountains, lush river valleys, and extensive orchards. Trails often feature a mix of well-paved surfaces and unpaved, technical sections, especially in areas like the Sierra de Ricote and along the Segura River. Ancient irrigation canals, known as acequias, also provide scenic paths through traditional agricultural areas.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Cieza?
A: Yes, Cieza offers trails for various skill levels. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are easier options available. For instance, the region has 3 easy routes out of a total of 59. Look for routes with less elevation gain and shorter distances if you're seeking a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or landmarks I can see while mountain biking in Cieza?
A: Cieza's trails are rich with natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de los Almacenes&lt;/a&gt;, where the Segura River carves a deep gorge, or visit the prehistoric &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sima de la Serreta Cave&lt;/a&gt; with its ancient rock paintings. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5051429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia de la Andelma&lt;/a&gt; offers a glimpse into traditional irrigation systems winding through orchards. You might also encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1345858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfonso XIII Reservoir Dam and Quípar River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cieza?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Cieza is generally during spring and autumn. Spring, particularly during 'La Floración' (Flowering in Cieza), transforms the almond and peach orchards into a spectacular display of colors, offering picturesque routes. Autumn provides pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winters are generally mild.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that offer significant elevation gain for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Cieza is known for its challenging terrain, especially in the Sierra de Ricote. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936473226/sierra-de-ricote-y-la-navela-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Ricote y La Navela Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 34.3-mile (55.3 km) trail, feature substantial elevation gains of over 1,300 meters, providing a demanding experience with panoramic views.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes around Cieza?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Cieza are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21863497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Alambre – Ricote Valley loop from Cieza&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10321800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Arenal River Beach – Casa Campana loop from Cieza&lt;/a&gt;, offering convenient options for exploration.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Cieza?
A: The mountain bike trails around Cieza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the region. Reviewers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the mix of challenging and moderate terrain, and the scenic beauty of the river valleys and orchards.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Segura River?
A: The Segura River is central to many outdoor activities in Cieza, and several mountain biking routes incorporate its scenic banks. The river's clear waters and surrounding landscapes provide excellent paths. For example, the 'Gorges, valleys and orchards — BTT along the Segura River (GR 127)' is a collection that highlights the river's appeal, and routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31414664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Presa River Beach – Puente de Alambre loop from Cieza&lt;/a&gt; often feature sections along the river.

Q: Are there any waterfalls near the mountain bike trails in Cieza?
A: Yes, you can find natural water features in the vicinity of Cieza's mountain bike trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2651129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Caputa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument that can be explored as part of a wider outdoor adventure in the region.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Cieza region?
A: The Cieza region offers a substantial network of mountain bike trails, with over 50 routes available. This includes a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult trails, ensuring options for various skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through the famous orchards of Cieza?
A: Yes, Cieza is renowned for its almond and peach orchards, and several routes wind through these picturesque agricultural areas. During spring, these orchards are particularly stunning due to 'La Floración'. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10321769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia de la Andelma – Puente de Alambre loop from Cieza&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that leads through ancient irrigation canals and orchards.
tips: Historical bathing place in summer, it is located just 1.5 kilometers from the town center. The beach is in good condition, since through the Environmental Volunteering program it is cared for by volunteers. Here you can interact with a wonderful nature

There were more than eighty kilometers of ditches of Arab or Roman origin that irrigated the Ciezana orchard, but today there are barely two kilometers left that preserve their traditional mud structure. The Andelma canal, whose name means "water source" runs along the river behind the Museo Molino de Teodoro, an old flour mill. Its historical, archaeological and ethnographic value is undoubted and it also has great ecological value - here we can find ivy, sarsaparilla, elms and maidenhair.

"El Menjú" is an old and abandoned farm, with a botanical garden included, located next to an old hydroelectric power station, also in disuse. It is an area of high ecological value, with more than 200 plant and animal species in the environment.
It is a beautiful specimen that is part of the Parque de las Norias de Abarán. It can still be seen turning and raising water after so many years in service. It's worth a visit.
There are four pretty waterwheels that continue to raise water and irrigate the surrounding orchards. The trail begins in the Parque de las Norias and goes through orchards and riverside forest.
Incredible views of one of the most vertical walls of the Ricote Valley

The Virgen del Buen Suceso is the patron saint of Cieza and in 1962 they built a small hermitage in her honor in the upper part of the Watchtower. There is a viewpoint with a cross and the views from here are spectacular. In September they take the Virgin to the Church of the Assumption and a week later they take her back to her hermitage.

It is a suspension bridge built in the middle of the 19th century over the Segura river. It has suffered several damages due to the floods, but today it is in perfect condition and also allows you to see the hill of La Atalaya. Without a doubt, a walk to remember!

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This is a demanding route, and it's estimated to take around 6 hours and 2 minutes to complete by mountain bike. This duration accounts for its significant length and elevation gain, so plan accordingly.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult for mountain biking. It requires a very good physical condition and considerable experience with mountain biking. Be prepared for sustained climbs, technical descents, and potentially sections where you might need to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging, typical of mountain trails in the Sierra de Ricote and La Navela areas. You can expect a mix of demanding ascents and descents over unpaved surfaces, which may include rocky paths, loose gravel, and narrow singletrack sections.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse and picturesque landscapes. Key highlights include the Viewpoint of the Hermitage of the Virgen del Buen Suceso, which provides spectacular panoramic views of Cieza. You'll also pass by the El Menjú Estate, the Noria of the Hoya de Don García, and the La Ñora Waterwheel, showcasing both natural beauty and historical structures.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, given the circular nature of the route starting and ending near Cieza, you would typically look for parking in or around the town of Cieza. Many riders start near the Ermita Virgen del Buen Suceso or other accessible points in the area. It's advisable to check local regulations for parking before your visit.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dogs on this specific trail is not explicitly provided. However, as parts of the route traverse the ZONA DE ESPECIAL PROTECCIÓN PARA AVES SIERRA DE RICOTE Y LA NAVELA, it's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially in protected natural areas. Always practice responsible pet ownership.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The Murcia region, where this trail is located, can experience very hot summers. Therefore, the best time to ride this trail would typically be during the cooler months of spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). Winter can also be pleasant, but always check the weather forecast for Cieza before heading out.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route, especially within the protected area?
faqs.answer: This route passes through the ZONA DE ESPECIAL PROTECCIÓN PARA AVES SIERRA DE RICOTE Y LA NAVELA (Special Protection Area for Birds). Generally, there are no entrance fees for accessing natural parks or protected areas for recreational activities like mountain biking in Spain, but specific activities might require permits. For mountain biking on established trails, permits are usually not required unless you are participating in an organized event or accessing private land. Always respect local regulations and signage within the protected zone.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official paths. Notable sections include parts of the Sendero del Collado de la Madera, Transmurciana 22, Río Segura, Camino de Santiago del Azahar, and Medina Siyasa (Cieza), among others. This offers opportunities to explore different parts of the region if you wish to extend or modify your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities or places to refuel along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is a long and challenging circular trail through natural environments. While it passes by some man-made highlights like waterwheels and estates, specific amenities like cafes or water refill points directly on the trail are not guaranteed. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water, snacks, and any necessary supplies for the entire duration of your ride, especially given the estimated 6-hour completion time. Cieza itself offers various amenities before and after your ride.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area without requiring an out-and-back return on the same path. This allows for a continuous and varied riding experience.

waypoints.name: Noria of the Hoya de Don García
Viewpoint of the Hermitage of the Virgen del Buen Suceso
